steps.instruction: Cut a gum wrapper into an hourglass shape.
steps.helpDetail: The foil side must be continuous. Cut the middle very thin (2mm). This creates high resistance.

steps.instruction: Touch the foil ends to the positive and negative terminals of a battery.
steps.helpDetail: Use an AA or AAA battery. The thin middle strip will heat up and ignite the paper backing instantly.

steps.instruction: Transfer the flame to your tinder bundle immediately.
steps.helpDetail: The flame lasts only a second. Have your tinder ready.

steps.instruction: Fluff up a piece of fine steel wool (Grade 0000 is best).
steps.helpDetail: Pull it apart to allow air flow.

steps.instruction: Touch the 9V battery terminals to the steel wool.
steps.helpDetail: Sparks will fly immediately and travel through the wool like a fuse.

steps.instruction: Blow on the glowing wool and add tinder.
steps.helpDetail: It burns very hot (3000°F). Do not hold it in your bare hand if possible.

steps.instruction: Remove lubricant if possible (wash with water/sand).
steps.helpDetail: Ideally use unlubricated, but in survival, hydration takes priority.

steps.instruction: Place the condom inside a sock or shirt sleeve BEFORE filling.
steps.helpDetail: Condoms are fragile when full. The sock provides structural support and protection from puncture.

steps.instruction: Fill carefully with water and tie off the end.
steps.helpDetail: Can hold up to 2 liters. Carry carefully by the sock/fabric, not the latex.

steps.instruction: Open tampon package. Fluff out the cotton material.
steps.helpDetail: It is sterile and packed with high-absorbency cotton. Great for plugging puncture wounds.

steps.instruction: Push into deep wounds or tape over surface cuts.
steps.helpDetail: For gunshot/puncture wounds, pack it inside. For cuts, flatten it out as a gaze pad.

steps.instruction: Secure with duct tape or fabric strip.
Cut two small strips of duct tape. Twist the middle of one strip.
steps.helpDetail: Twisting the middle creates a non-sticky bridge that goes over the wound.

steps.instruction: Stick one end to skin, pull wound closed, then stick other end.
steps.helpDetail: Actively pull the skin edges together before securing the second side.

steps.instruction: Method 2: Cut 'H' or Butterfly shape from tape.
steps.helpDetail: Cut notches in the sides of a strip to make a narrow bridge.

techniques.name: CD Signal Mirror
techniques.subtitle: Use old disc for rescue signaling

steps.instruction: Use the shiny side of a CD or DVD.
steps.helpDetail: Data side reflects light better than the label side.

steps.instruction: Look through the center hole at your target (plane/boat).
steps.helpDetail: The hole acts as an aiming sight.

steps.instruction: Flash the sunlight across the target repeatedly.
steps.helpDetail: Tilt the CD back and forth to sweep the beam across the rescuer's field of view.
